paper_title: World Allergy Organization Study on Aerobiology for Creating First Pollen and Mold Calendar With Clinical Significance in Islamabad, Pakistan

methods.description: Process for collecting airborne pollen and mold spores using a volumetric spore trap over a period of three years to create a pollen and mold calendar.

steps.description: The spore trap was placed at the height of 11 m and was allowed to run continuously.
steps.searchable_summary: set up and operate spore trap at 11 m height

steps.description: Every week, the collecting drum was prepared with Melinex tape fastened around the drum with double-sided adhesive tape joining both ends of Melinex tape, which is then coated with a thin layer of silicone grease.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare collecting drum with Melinex tape and silicone grease

steps.description: The drum was removed on every Sunday at 9:00 AM and replaced with another drum coated as above at the same time.
Permanent slides were prepared and particles (pollens and mold spores) counted according to recommended protocol.
Pollen and mold spores were counted with ·400 magnification and recorded as counts per cubic meter of air per hour.
steps.searchable_summary: record spore counts per cubic meter per hour
outputs: spore counts per cubic meter per hour

steps.description: From the data of 3 years (2005, 2006, and 2007), pollen and mold calendars were prepared per cubic meter of air per day.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare pollen and mold calendars from 3-year data

steps.description: The data were rounded off and came up with average counts per cubic meter of air per hour per month.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate average spore counts per hour per month

methods.description: Procedure for conducting skin prick tests to assess allergic reactions to pollens, molds, and house dust.

steps.description: Skin prick tests (SPTs) were done with the extracts purchased from Hollister-Steir (Spokane, WA).
SPTs were done for pollens and molds and house dust and results were recorded.
